title: Engineering technicians
description: Design, develop, test, and evaluate integrated systems for managing industrial production processes, including human work factors, quality control, inventory control, logistics and material flow, cost analysis, and production coordination. Design objects, facilities, and environments to optimize human well-being and overall system performance, applying theory, principles, and data regarding the relationship between humans and respective technology. Investigate and analyze characteristics of human behavior and performance as it relates to the use of technology. Design or plan protocols for equipment or processes to produce products meeting internal and external purity, safety, and quality requirements. (Based on 28 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Confer with clients, vendors, staff, and management regarding purchases, product specifications, manufacturing capabilities, or project status.
Recommend resolution of identified deviations from established product or process standards.
Assess the user-interface or usability characteristics of products and advocate for end users in collaboration with other professionals.
Collect data through direct observation of work activities or witnessing the conduct of tests, and conduct interviews or surveys of users or customers.
Apply modeling or quantitative analysis to forecast events, such as human decisions or behaviors, and analyze complex systems for development or compatibility.
Evaluate or design human work systems using human factors engineering and ergonomic principles to optimize usability, safety, or performance.
Design tests of finished products or process capabilities to establish standards or validate process requirements; recommend changes in designs or methods using statistical procedures.
Incorporate new manufacturing methods to improve existing operations and identify opportunities to enhance manufacturing processes or reduce costs.
Evaluate current or proposed manufacturing processes for environmental sustainability and conduct research to evaluate changes related to equipment or manpower.
Design cognitive aids and develop research methodologies to evaluate developmental prototypes for new products or processes.
Inspect work sites to identify physical hazards and recommend workplace changes to improve health and safety based on ergonomic principles.
Estimate time or resource requirements for ergonomic research or development projects and prepare reports on human factors engineering activities.
Train users in task techniques or ergonomic principles and assist in training equipment operators on validation protocols and standard operating procedures.
Prepare, maintain, or review validation and compliance documentation, and direct validation activities to ensure compliance with standards.
Conduct validation tests of new or existing processes or equipment according to internal protocols and analyze data to determine if validation criteria are met.
Design layout of equipment or workspaces for efficiency and communicate with management to develop production and design standards.
Evaluate manufactured products against specifications and quality standards, and troubleshoot designs or procedures for improvements.
Prepare detailed reports based on validation and qualification tests or reviews of procedures and establish quality control objectives to resolve production issues.
Develop manufacturing methods, labor utilization standards, or cost analysis systems to enhance operational efficiency.
Modify, maintain, or repair electronics equipment or systems and assist engineers in the design and testing of machinery or processes.
Monitor, inspect, or test mechanical equipment and review project instructions to ascertain test specifications, procedures, and objectives.
Set up mechanical, hydraulic, or electric test equipment in accordance with engineering specifications, standards, or test procedures.
Recommend tests or testing conditions based on designs, customer requirements, or industry standards to ensure test validity.
Read and interpret blueprints, schematics, work specifications, drawings, or charts to perform tasks accurately.
Review project instructions and specifications to identify, modify, and plan fabrication, assembly, and testing requirements.
Maintain test equipment in operational condition by performing routine maintenance and minor repairs as needed.
Inspect or test parts to determine the nature or cause of defects or malfunctions and document findings.
Analyze test results in relation to design specifications and modify or adjust equipment to meet requirements.
Fabricate new or modify existing prototype components or fixtures using hand tools, power tools, and test instruments.
Conduct tests of complete units and components under operational conditions to investigate proposals for improving performance.
Operates and calibrate computer systems and devices to comply with test requirements for data acquisition and analysis.
Document robotics test procedures and results, including numerical and graphical data, and recommendations for changes.
Perform preventive or corrective maintenance on robotic systems or components to ensure proper functioning and reliability.
Select electromechanical equipment, materials, components, or systems to meet functional specifications and compliance requirements.
Analyze proposed site factors and design maps, graphs, tracings, and diagrams to illustrate findings effectively.
Inspect and monitor construction sites to ensure adherence to safety standards, building codes, or specifications.
Identify and prioritize energy-saving measures, and educate customers on energy efficiency improvements.
Collect and analyze field data related to energy usage, including the identification of opportunities to improve energy efficiency.
Calculate dimensions, material quantities, and energy consumption to establish baselines and compliance with specifications.
Operate and test parts and assemblies to verify performance, utilizing diagnostic techniques to ensure functionality.
Prepare audit reports containing energy analysis results or recommendations for energy cost savings.

emerging_tasks.task: Assess systems to identify and quantify risk factors.
Operate, test, and troubleshoot uncrewed aerial systems, commonly known as drones, to ensure optimal performance.
Program and calibrate drones for specific missions or tasks, ensuring proper functionality and performance.
Evaluate the energy performance of buildings using modeling software.
Inspect structures to determine cause and origin of damage.
Operate drones for site surveying and inspection, providing detailed aerial views of project sites.
Use drone technology to capture aerial images or videos for creating detailed and accurate maps.
Deploy and monitor drones for aerial surveillance and assessment of fire situations.
Operate drones for remote inspection of large or hard-to-reach structures, such as wind turbines, bridges, or tall buildings.
